title: ficus austrocaledonica var. austrocaledonica
intro: Ficus austrocaledonica var. austrocaledonica, also known as the Vanuatu fig tree, is a species of fig tree native to the island of Espiritu Santo in Vanuatu, characterized by its small size and broad, rounded leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of various health issues. The health benefits of Ficus austrocaledonica var. austrocaledonica include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and potentially lowering blood pressure, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
